title: Widgets and Character Mode
text: Widgets and Character Mode If you create components that are intended for use in a character mode environment, be economical with the amount of text you display in each label and widget. Labels and widgets can contain proportional fonts of varying size, which makes it easy to fit a large amount of text into a small space in a GUI environment. However, the same text often occupies more space in a character mode environment. Uniface truncates text that does not fit. To prevent truncation, test the text by temporarily converting it to a non-proportional font (such as Courier), making sure that it still fits in the label or widget. Was this topic helpful?
